bull_thesis: Prosegur is a three-division physical-security conglomerate trading at 5.5 times trailing twelve-month free cash flow and a roughly 4.9% dividend yield, even as every operating division is turning up at the same time. The supporting facts are concrete, not narrative: net income rose 52.8% from EUR 78 million to EUR 119 million in full-year 2025, earnings per share climbed 46.7% from EUR 0.15 to EUR 0.22, operating earnings before interest and amortisation (EBITA) grew 8.9% to EUR 357 million, and trailing twelve-month free cash flow reached EUR 218 million — up 53% year over year — against reported revenue that was essentially flat (+0.5%). That is pure operating leverage and a lower effective tax rate (42.0%, down 4.49 percentage points year over year), not a demand windfall. Reported growth is being suppressed by the translation of Argentine pesos and US dollars into euros; organic growth was +10% in full-year 2025 and +7.8% in the first quarter of 2026. Three divisions, three potential re-rating paths: (1) Cash — the roughly 74%-owned listed subsidiary Prosegur Cash (ticker CASH.MC) is a Brink's-grade cash-in-transit franchise earning an 11.3% EBITA margin, with a growing share of recurring 'transformation products' now at 36.4% of Cash division sales; the market already values this subsidiary at 8.9 times EBITDA (enterprise value divided by earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation) and a EUR 1.83 billion enterprise value. (2) Security — EUR 2.7 billion in revenue, which crossed cash-flow breakeven in the first quarter 'for the first time in a first quarter', with US operations growing at double-digit rates and the Cipher cyber unit folded in to reposition the division as integrated physical-plus-digital security. (3) Alarms — a genuine subscription compounder: the subscriber base grew 8.9% to 1.07 million, customer cancellation rates (churn) fell from 95 to 67 basis points (each basis point is one-hundredth of a percentage point), the service margin expanded from 21% to 25%, and activation-fee revenue jumped 35%. Debt reduction is progressing (net-debt-to-EBITDA of 2.4 times, down EUR 28 million quarter over quarter, investment-grade BBB rating, more than 60% of debt fixed at 2.9% average cost). Management guidance is credible — five beats in seven reported quarters, and full-year 2025 beat the company's own target; point estimates for the second and third quarters of 2026 are EUR 1.44 billion and EUR 1.49 billion respectively. At 5.5 times free cash flow you are paid to own a deleveraging, earnings-inflecting cash-generating business alongside an aligned controlling family. If free cash flow holds at EUR 218 million and the market pays even 9 to 10 times (a discount to peer Securitas at roughly 12 to 14 times), the equity is worth EUR 1.96 billion to EUR 2.18 billion against roughly EUR 1.45 billion today — a 35 to 50% re-rating while collecting roughly 4.9%.
bear_thesis: Prosegur is a structurally low-quality, foreign-exchange-cursed, thin-margin security-guard conglomerate that deserves its market discount — and has no catalyst to close it. Net margin is 2.4%, return on invested capital (ROIC, the profit the business earns on every dollar deployed) is 3.7%, and the Security division EBITA margin is just 2.8%. That is a labour-arbitrage guard business with razor-thin economics; no amount of 'integrated cyber' branding earns a technology-company multiple on 2.8% margins. The growth story is entirely organic — in reported euros, which is the only currency shareholders are actually paid in, revenue is flat (+0.5% for the full year, +1.5% in the first quarter) because Argentine and US dollar exposure repeatedly consumes it. Argentina is a recurring, uncontrollable wound: Cash division EBITA was 'negatively impacted by Argentina macroeconomic conditions', and one more Argentine peso devaluation could flatten the reported profit line again. The earnings-per-share improvement is lower quality than it appears — much of it reflects a lower tax rate and operating leverage off a depressed base — and management itself acknowledged that margin gains 'will be less significant over the course of the year'. Leverage is heavy: EUR 1.62 billion in net debt against EUR 119 million in net income; paying down debt at roughly EUR 28 million per quarter is glacial. Most damaging for a deep-value argument: there is no catalyst that forces a re-rating. The only structural event — the Gubel family tender offer — closed in March 2024 at EUR 1.83 per share, which is below today's EUR 2.68, and since the Gubel family already controls roughly 75%, the event that could have triggered a re-rating already fired in the wrong direction. Minority shareholders are at the mercy of a controlling family with no obligation to surface value, no announced buyback acceleration, no plan to take the Cash subsidiary private, no asset sale, no outside activist (the ratio of insider buys to sells is zero). A listed holding company that owns a separately listed subsidiary is a permanent discount-to-sum-of-the-parts machine; that discount rarely closes without an unannounced corporate action. The stock stays a 5.5-times-free-cash-flow value trap; you collect 4.9% while waiting indefinitely.
consensus_delta: For a deep-value stock, the key question is whether the market is pricing in terminal decline when the business actually has stable or growing earnings power. Here the market is not badly wrong — it is pricing a deserved quality discount and is roughly correct on direction, if not magnitude. Where the market is underweighting: the quality of the full-year 2025 earnings improvement (net income +52.8%, free cash flow +53%) is real and durable — driven by operating leverage, a permanently lower tax rate, and the Alarms division's shift toward subscriptions — not a one-off event, yet the stock still trades at 5.5 times free cash flow as if that cash flow were cyclically elevated. The market's mistake is treating EUR 218 million in consolidated free cash flow as fragile, when roughly 36% of the Cash division's revenue is now recurring 'transformation products' and Alarms churn has structurally fallen from 95 to 67 basis points. Where the market is right (and the bull case must concede): reported revenue is flat, Argentine peso exposure is a recurring drag on the reported line, and there is no catalyst — so the discount can persist for years. The disagreement with the market is modest, not dramatic: a re-rating of the free-cash-flow multiple from 5.5 times toward 8 times as earnings quality is recognised, not a special-situation re-rating all the way to fair value.
forcing_function: There is no hard-dated catalyst that forces a re-rating — this is the thesis's central weakness. The next discrete events are ordinary earnings releases (the second quarter of 2026, expected in roughly July or August, approximately 48 days away at the time of writing), which can confirm the free-cash-flow and margin trajectory but are not re-rating catalysts on their own. A genuine catalyst that forces a re-rating would require an unannounced corporate action: a take-private or squeeze-out of the listed Prosegur Cash subsidiary (since the Gubel family already controls the parent, consolidating the listed subsidiary would collapse the holding-company discount), a meaningful share buyback programme, a sale of the Cash or Alarms stake, or entry by an outside activist investor — none of which are on the calendar as of 13 June 2026. Without one of those, the re-rating depends on slow free-cash-flow multiple convergence over several quarters. The honest answer: watch the next one or two earnings prints for currency-clean free-cash-flow confirmation and any shift in capital-allocation strategy (buyback or Cash consolidation); enter on a price flush, not because a catalyst has arrived, because the catalyst does not yet exist.
valley_of_death: The dominant risk over the next three to nine months is an Argentine peso (and secondarily US dollar) devaluation that flattens the reported profit-and-loss statement again — the Cash division EBITA was already 'negatively impacted by Argentina macroeconomic conditions' in the first quarter of 2026, and a sharp peso move would erase the reported growth narrative and likely the stock's recent gains, even if the underlying organic numbers stayed healthy. Second, Security margin disappointment: management already signalled that margin gains 'will be less significant over the course of the year', so a second- or third-quarter print showing the 2.8% EBITA margin stalling would puncture the 'structural margin transformation' story. Third, debt reduction is slow (roughly EUR 28 million per quarter against EUR 1.62 billion in net debt); any working-capital reversal — the first-quarter swing to positive EUR 8 million operating cash flow from negative EUR 19 million in the prior year is seasonally fragile — could stall the debt-reduction narrative. There is no debt-maturity cliff (the business carries an investment-grade BBB rating, more than 60% of debt is at a fixed 2.9% average cost, a EUR 60 million European Investment Bank loan was recently secured, and the EUR 600 million Cash subsidiary bond has already been amortised), so the risk is earnings and foreign exchange, not refinancing. There is no period of negative cash generation ahead — the business is free-cash-flow positive.
positioning_washout: There is limited potential for forced-selling pressure. The dividend (roughly EUR 0.13 per share, roughly 4.9% yield, roughly 77% payout ratio) is stable and has not been cut, so there is no income-investor capitulation trigger of the kind that emerges when a dividend is reduced. The more significant dynamic is actually the opposite of forced selling: the Gubel-Revoredo family controls roughly 75%, leaving a free float of only about 25% — thin and relatively illiquid. That thin float both limits the potential for large-scale forced selling and, critically, removes any large mechanical buyer who could drive a re-rating on the upside. There is no index-inclusion event pending, no known forced-seller situation, and no visible activist accumulation (the ratio of insider buys to sells is zero). The controlled-company, thin-float structure is a double-edged feature: it puts a floor under downside (no large holder is likely to dump the stock) but simultaneously eliminates any positioning-driven catalyst on the upside.
moderator_conclusion: PSG.MC is a thorough and credible research case on a low-quality business with genuine but slow-burning cheapness and no catalyst. The research analyst rated management credibility at 4 out of 5, with the trajectory strengthening — earnings up 52.8%, free cash flow up 53%, Alarms unit economics improving, and debt reduction all genuinely on track; management is confident and specific about what it controls. The valuation analyst's sum-of-the-parts range brackets EUR 1.90 at the cautious end (strict break-up value, currency-impaired) to EUR 3.60 at the optimistic end (free-cash-flow anchored); the reconciled base case is roughly EUR 2.85, a thin 6% premium to EUR 2.68 once the deserved discounts for thin margins (2.8% Security EBITA, 3.7% return on invested capital) and the holding-company and foreign-exchange penalties are applied. The model's implied margin of safety of 75 to 81% is too optimistic at current prices. Applying the current investment regime, which rewards hard-dated structural special situations in under-covered, distressed, deleveraging, or forced-seller situations and explicitly avoids theses with no dated catalyst or that depend on slow multiple convergence: Prosegur's only corporate event — the Gubel family tender offer in 2024 at EUR 1.83, below today's price — has already fired; the family already owns roughly 75%, leaving a thin float and no mechanical re-rating buyer; and there is no announced buyback, take-private, or asset sale. On the catalyst dimension, the regime moves this name down. The recommendation is 'wait for the wash-out', conviction 2: do not buy at EUR 2.68. The right entry is a capitulation flush — an Argentine-peso-devaluation-driven reported-revenue scare that drives the price toward the bear-case break-up value of roughly EUR 1.90 — at which point you would own a deleveraging EUR 218 million free-cash-flow business at roughly 4 times free cash flow and roughly a 6% yield. Separately, stripped of the catalyst and regime filter, the pure value case is strong (conviction 4): genuinely cheap at 5.5 times free cash flow, genuinely inflecting in earnings and cash generation, well financed (BBB-rated), with high research credibility (4 of 5). The only thing capping the value score at 4 rather than 5 is the structurally low return on invested capital and margins, plus the foreign-exchange fragility, which make part of the discount legitimate. The two scores diverge deliberately: cheap and clean on value (4), but no catalyst and in an out-of-favour lane for the current regime (conviction 2).
sop_breakdown: The base-case sum-of-the-parts (valuing each business line separately) blends the free-cash-flow-anchored consolidated value — which captures the real EUR 218 million cash machine — against the stricter segment-EBITA-minus-leverage breakup value, which respects the deserved thin-margin and holding-company discount, then applies a haircut for foreign-exchange risk and the absence of a dated catalyst. Parts at base-case multiples: the 74% stake in the Cash subsidiary, roughly EUR 0.78 billion (split between intrinsic EUR 0.84 billion and the market value of EUR 0.72 billion); Security, roughly EUR 0.54 billion (7 times approximately EUR 77 million in EBITA); Alarms, roughly EUR 0.36 billion (1.4 times sales). Gross value roughly EUR 1.68 billion, less EUR 0.76 billion parent net debt, leaves approximately EUR 0.92 billion of equity value — or roughly EUR 1.70 per share on a strict break-up basis. The consolidated free-cash-flow lens (EUR 218 million times approximately 8 times equals EUR 1.74 billion equity value, or EUR 3.23 per share) lifts fair value because Prosegur's worth is in aggregate cash generation, not easily separable parts. Weighting the consolidated view roughly 60% and the break-up view roughly 40%, with a modest foreign-exchange and holding-company haircut, lands the base-case fair value at EUR 2.85 per share — a slim 6% premium to EUR 2.68. The business is cheap, but the deserved discount consumes most of the headline 75 to 81% model-implied margin of safety.
risk_reward: The margin of safety is thin, and the risk-reward profile is not asymmetric. Downside to the bear-case break-up value of EUR 1.90 represents a loss of 29% from EUR 2.68; upside to base-case fair value of EUR 2.85 is only 6%; upside to the bull case of EUR 3.60 is 34%. The base-case risk-to-reward ratio is roughly 0.2-to-1 (poor); the bull-case ratio is roughly 1.2-to-1. The model's implied margin of safety of 75 to 81% (on Graham and intrinsic-value-based methods) overstates the edge: once the thin-margin, holding-company, and foreign-exchange discounts are properly reflected, realistic upside to fair value is in the single digits at base and roughly 34% only in a scenario with no currency shock. This is a 'cheap with a thin margin of safety' situation, not an asymmetric special opportunity.
catalyst_status: FIRED. The only structural catalyst -- the Gubel, S.L. (Revoredo family) tender offer -- was authorized by CNMV on 2024-02-20 and completed 2024-03-18 at EUR1.83/share for ~13.23% of capital, lifting Gubel to ~74.8% ownership. That event is >2 years old AND priced BELOW today's EUR2.68, so the re-rate it could have driven is spent and went the wrong way for minorities. Verified as of 2026-06-13: NO live tender/squeeze-out, NO announced buyback ramp, NO Prosegur Cash take-private/delisting, NO asset sale, NO activist (insider buy ratio 0.00); the Q1-2026 call disclosed zero capital-action plans beyond routine deleveraging. The thesis rests entirely on FCF-multiple convergence with no dated forcing function.
peer_comps_note: The true comparable companies are global contract-security firms — primarily Securitas (ticker SECU-B on the Stockholm exchange) and the private businesses G4S/Allied Universal — along with its own listed Cash subsidiary (Prosegur Cash, ticker CASH.MC) and Brink's Company (ticker BCO) for cash-logistics benchmarking. At 5.5 times price-to-free-cash-flow, Prosegur screens as cheap against Securitas at roughly 12 to 14 times free cash flow. However, a return on invested capital of 3.7% and a net margin of 2.4% mean that part of the discount is earned — it reflects Latin American currency drag and the structurally thin margins of manned guarding. The holding-company multiple check: the listed Cash subsidiary already trades at 8.9 times EBITDA and a EUR 1.83 billion enterprise value (versus Brink's at roughly 6.5 times), so the crown-jewel division is not cheap on its own market price — it is the consolidated holding company that carries the discount. Conclusion: cheap versus peers on a free-cash-flow basis, but the gap to Securitas is at least partly justified by quality differences, not pure mispricing.
interrogator_dossier:
Prosegur Compañía de Seguridad, S.A. (PSG.MC) — Research Dossier

Prepared by the research analyst (Claude Opus 4.8). Sources: Prosegur full-year 2025 results and press release (prosegur.com); fourth-quarter 2025 and first-quarter 2026 earnings calls and slides (Investing.com transcripts); company investor-relations materials; Spanish securities regulator (CNMV) history covering the Gubel tender offer. No earnings-call transcript was bundled directly; this dossier reasons from the screener financial data cross-referenced against the most recent management commentary found on the web. Direct quotes are management phrasings as reported in first-quarter and fourth-quarter 2026 call coverage; numbers from the screener data block are labelled '(financials)'. Latest reported quarter: first quarter of fiscal year 2026 (ended 31 March 2026).

1. Narrative Arc

Prosegur is a three-division physical-security conglomerate — Security (manned guarding plus technology), Cash (cash-in-transit and cash management, held via the roughly 74%-owned listed subsidiary Prosegur Cash, ticker CASH.MC), and Alarms (residential and small-business monitoring). Management's story across the past several quarters is consistent and, unusually for a deep-value name, improving: a multi-year 'value creation for shareholders' deleveraging-and-margin-recovery narrative.

How the narrative has evolved: the older framing was dominated by Latin American currency as an existential drag masking real volume growth. By full-year 2025 the message shifted to 'organic growth is the truth, foreign exchange is noise' — management now leads every release with organic figures (full-year 2025 organic +10%, first quarter of 2026 organic +7.8%) and treats the reported number (full-year 2025 +0.5%, first quarter of 2026 +1.5%) as a translation artefact. The newest thread, introduced in the first quarter of 2026, is structural margin transformation: in the Cash division, 'transformation products now represent 36.4% of total Cash sales' (recurring, higher-margin services replacing volume-linked cash-in-transit work); in Alarms, a deliberate pivot from 'grow the base at any cost' to quality growth (churn cut from 95 basis points to 67 basis points, service margin up from 21% to 25%). The Security division crossed a symbolic line in the first quarter of 2026: 'Security reached cash flow breakeven for the first time in a first quarter', and the Cipher cybersecurity unit was folded into Security to position the division as an integrated physical-and-digital security provider.

Drivers quietly dropped: the old emphasis on top-line cash-in-transit volume in the Cash division has been replaced by 'transformation products' — a tacit acknowledgement that legacy cash logistics is ex-growth and the real bet is a mix shift toward services. Drivers added: double-digit growth in US Security, Alarms unit economics, and balance-sheet deleveraging as the headline value driver.

2. Claims Versus Financials

Management claim: 'Strong, broad-based growth.' Hard evidence: reported revenue full-year 2025 +0.5%, first quarter of 2026 +1.5%; three-year compound annual growth rate +5.7%; organic growth +10% and +7.8% respectively. Assessment: half-true. The organic growth is genuine; reported growth is near-zero because foreign-exchange translation (Argentine peso, US dollar) erases it. Shareholders are paid in reported euros, not organic figures.

Management claim: 'On the right track — generating value for shareholders' — an earnings improvement. Hard evidence: net income EUR 78 million (2024) rising to EUR 119 million (2025), +52.8%; earnings per share EUR 0.15 rising to EUR 0.22, +46.7% year over year; EBITA EUR 357 million, +8.9%. Assessment: true and material. This is the load-bearing fact. Earnings power improves sharply while revenue is flat — the result of operating leverage and a permanently lower effective tax rate (42.0%, down 4.49 percentage points year over year).

Management claim: margin expansion. Hard evidence: net margin 2.4% (financials); gross margin 20.4%, stable; consolidated EBITDA margin 11.1%; EBITA margin roughly 6.8%; Security EBITA margin 2.8%. Assessment: mixed. Group margins are structurally thin (manned guarding is a 2 to 3% operating-earnings business). Expansion at the EBITA line is real (+8.9%) but off a low base; the net margin remains razor-thin.

Management claim: debt reduction. Hard evidence: net debt EUR 1.62 billion (financials, as of 31 March 2026); group-reported EUR 1.381 billion; net debt to EBITDA 2.4 times, down EUR 28 million quarter over quarter; target below 2.4 times by year-end. Assessment: true, but slow. Debt reduction is under way at a modest pace; EUR 1.6 billion in net debt against EUR 119 million in net income is still heavy leverage.

Management claim: free cash flow recovery. Hard evidence: trailing twelve-month free cash flow EUR 218 million (financials); price-to-free-cash-flow 5.5 times; free cash flow +53.3% year over year; first-quarter 2026 operating cash flow positive EUR 8 million versus negative EUR 19 million in the prior year (web). Assessment: true. Second load-bearing fact — free cash flow is real, growing, and 5.5 times is genuinely cheap if it can be sustained. The first quarter turning cash-positive is a structural seasonality break.

Where the story is stronger than the numbers: the 'value creation' framing implies a re-rating is owed; nothing in the flat reported revenue supports a growth re-rating — only the earnings and free-cash-flow improvement does. Where it is weaker: management understates how much of the earnings-per-share jump reflects a lower tax rate and operating leverage rather than demand, and how exposed the business is to one more Argentine peso devaluation.

3. Management Tone and Confidence

Rising and stable — confident, not defensive. Across the fourth quarter of 2025 and the first quarter of 2026 the tone is on the front foot: 'a strong first quarter', 'solid operations performance', 'broad-based growth across regions', 'on the right track to comply with our main objective.' Management volunteers specific forward revenue figures (second quarter EUR 1.44 billion, third quarter EUR 1.49 billion) — a confidence signal; managements that have lost their growth story give ranges, not point estimates. Language is operational and granular (cancellation rate in basis points, transformation-product percentage, days of sales outstanding) rather than heavy on macro excuses, a positive sign for a business with Latin American exposure. The one defensive note is foreign exchange, handled with 'robust risk management practices' but no disclosed hedge ratios.

4. Guidance Credibility

Medium-high. Financials show beats in 5 of 7 quarters (71%) and full-year 2025 net profit 'exceeding targets' (company headline). Earnings per share compounded at +22.4% over three years and the +52.8% net-profit jump beat the company's own full-year 2025 target. Management provides specific revenue forecasts and broadly delivers — first-quarter 2026 net profit +15.2% continues the streak. The reliability gap is foreign exchange: management cannot credibly guide reported-euro numbers (they do not control the peso), so they have shifted to organic and earnings metrics, where they do deliver. Rating: medium-high on what is within management control; low on reported-euro predictability.

5. Analyst Pressure Points

First, foreign-exchange and earnings sensitivity — when pressed for a quantified foreign-exchange impact, management offered 'robust risk management practices' with no hedge ratios, which is a non-answer. Second, Security margin sustainability — when pushed on whether the 2.8% EBITA margin would keep climbing, the chief financial officer acknowledged that improvements 'will be less significant over the course of the year', meaning the easy gains are behind. Third, the Cash division and Argentina — Cash EBITA was flat and 'negatively impacted by Argentina macroeconomic conditions', which is the structural soft underbelly. What analysts are not pressing on but should: the absence of any catalyst to close the holding-company and sum-of-the-parts discount (no buyback acceleration, no Cash take-private, no asset sale).

6. Positive and Negative Signals

Positive signals: net income +52.8% in full-year 2025, earnings per share +46.7% — a genuine earnings improvement, not financial engineering (corroborated by EBITA +8.9% and tax normalisation). Free cash flow EUR 218 million on a trailing twelve-month basis at 5.5 times; first-quarter 2026 turned operating-cash-positive (positive EUR 8 million versus negative EUR 19 million in the prior year). Alarms unit economics improving (churn from 95 to 67 basis points, service margin from 21% to 25%). Debt reduction on track (2.4 times net debt to EBITDA, down EUR 28 million quarter over quarter), BBB-rated, more than 60% of debt fixed at 2.9% average cost.

Negative signals: net margin 2.4%, Security EBITA margin 2.8%, return on invested capital 3.7% — structurally low quality; the business deserves a discount. Reported revenue flat (+0.5% for the full year, +1.5% in the first quarter) — the growth story lives in the organic figures, which foreign exchange repeatedly erases. EUR 1.6 billion in net debt is heavy; Argentina is a recurring, uncontrollable drag on the Cash division. No catalyst: the only structural event — the Gubel family tender offer at EUR 1.83 per share — closed in March 2024 below today's EUR 2.68. No live buyback acceleration, no Cash take-private, no asset sale, no activist. The ratio of insider buys to sells is zero.

7. Hidden Signals

The terminology shift from 'volume' to 'transformation products' in the Cash division is the key signal — it concedes that legacy cash-in-transit is in secular decline and reframes the business as a services and margin story rather than a volume story. The integration of the Cipher cybersecurity unit into Security signals that Prosegur wants to be valued as an integrated security platform, not a labour-arbitrage guard company — a multiple-aspiration move, though the 2.8% margin tells you the market is not yet paying for it. The ritual invocation of 'value for shareholders' without any accompanying capital-return action (a flat dividend of roughly EUR 0.13 per share, roughly 4.9% yield, no buyback acceleration) reads as aspiration rather than plan — management knows the stock is cheap and is signalling it, but has no mechanism to force the gap closed.

8. Capital-Allocation Assessment

Management credibility 4 of 5; trajectory turning stronger. The operating trajectory is genuinely improving — earnings, free cash flow, Alarms unit economics, and debt reduction all point in the right direction, and management is credible and specific about what it controls. However, this is a quality-improving but still low-quality business with no catalyst to force the discount closed. The single most important thing to weigh: this is a pure margin-of-safety and free-cash-flow multiple bet, not a special situation. The Gubel family tender at EUR 1.83 has already fired — below today's price — the family already owns roughly 75%, and there is no announced buyback programme, Cash take-private, or asset sale to act as a forcing function. You are paid 5.5 times free cash flow and a roughly 4.9% dividend to wait for a re-rating that has no dated trigger and is perpetually one Argentine peso devaluation away from being wiped off the reported income statement.

bull_thesis: Prosegur is a three-engine physical-security conglomerate trading at 5.5x TTM FCF and ~4.9% dividend yield while every operating engine is inflecting up. The load-bearing facts are hard, not narrative: net income +52.8% (EUR78M->EUR119M FY2025), EPS +46.7% (EUR0.15->EUR0.22), EBITA +8.9% to EUR357M, and TTM FCF EUR218M (+53% YoY) against FLAT reported revenue (+0.5%) -- pure operating leverage plus tax-rate normalization (effective tax 42.0%, -449bps YoY), not a demand sugar-high. Reported growth is depressed by ARS/USD translation; organic growth is +10% FY2025 / +7.8% Q1-2026. Three engines, three re-rate paths: (1) CASH -- the ~74%-owned listed Prosegur Cash (CASH.MC) is a Brink's-grade CIT franchise at 11.3% EBITA margin with mix-shift to recurring 'transformation products' now 36.4% of Cash sales; the market already values Cash at 8.9x EBITDA / EUR1.83B EV. (2) SECURITY -- EUR2.7B revenue, crossed Q1 cash-flow breakeven 'for the first time in a first quarter', US double-digit, Cipher cyber folded in to reposition as integrated physical+digital security. (3) ALARMS -- a real subscription compounder: base +8.9% to 1.07M, churn cut 95->67bps, service margin 21%->25%, activation-fee revenue +35%. Deleveraging on track (2.4x, -EUR28M QoQ, BBB, 60%+ fixed at 2.9%). Guidance credible (5/7 beats, FY2025 beat its own target, point Q2/Q3 guides EUR1.44B/EUR1.49B). At 5.5x FCF you are paid to own a deleveraging, earnings-inflecting cash machine alongside an aligned controlling family. If FCF holds at EUR218M and the market pays even 9-10x (a discount to Securitas ~12-14x), equity is worth EUR1.96-2.18B vs ~EUR1.45B today -- a 35-50% re-rate while collecting ~4.9%.
bear_thesis: Prosegur is a structurally low-quality, FX-cursed, low-margin manned-guarding conglomerate that DESERVES its discount and has NO catalyst to close it. Net margin 2.4%, ROIC 3.7%, Security EBITA margin just 2.8% -- a labor-arbitrage guard business with razor-thin economics; no amount of 'integrated cyber' branding earns a tech multiple on 2.8% margins. The growth story is entirely 'organic'; in reported euros -- the only currency shareholders are paid in -- revenue is FLAT (+0.5% FY, +1.5% Q1) because ARS/USD repeatedly eat it. Argentina is a recurring, uncontrollable wound: Cash EBITA was 'negatively impacted by Argentina macroeconomic conditions' and one more peso devaluation flattens the reported line again. The EPS inflection is lower-quality than it looks -- much is tax normalization and operating leverage off a depressed base -- and management conceded margin gains 'will be less significant over the course of the year'. Leverage is heavy: EUR1.62B net debt vs EUR119M net income; deleveraging at ~EUR28M/quarter is glacial. Most damning for a deep-value thesis: NO forcing function. The only structural event -- Gubel's tender -- closed March 2024 at EUR1.83/share, BELOW today's EUR2.68, and Gubel already controls ~75%, so the cheap re-rate event already fired in the wrong direction and minorities are at the mercy of a controlling family with no obligation to surface value, no announced buyback ramp, no Cash take-private, no asset sale, no activist (insider buy ratio 0.00). A listed parent owning a listed sub is a permanent SoP-discount machine that rarely closes without an unannounced corporate action. The stock stays a 5.5x-FCF value trap; you collect 4.9% to wait indefinitely.
consensus_delta: For a deep_value name the question is whether the street prices terminal decline into stable/growing earnings power. Here the street is NOT badly offside -- it prices a deserved quality discount and is roughly right on direction if not magnitude. The genuine mispricing the consensus underweights: the QUALITY of the FY2025 earnings inflection (+52.8% net income, +53% FCF) is real and durable (operating leverage + permanent tax-rate normalization + Alarms subscription mix-shift), not a one-off, yet the stock still trades at 5.5x FCF as if FCF were peak/cyclical. The street's error is treating consolidated EUR218M FCF as fragile when ~36% of Cash is now recurring 'transformation products' and Alarms churn has structurally fallen 95->67bps. Where the street is RIGHT (and the bull must concede): reported revenue is flat, ARS is a recurring tax on the reported line, and there is no catalyst -- so the discount can persist for years. The expectations arbitrage is modest, not violent: a quality-of-earnings re-rate from 5.5x toward 8x FCF, not a special-situation re-rate to fair value.
forcing_function: There is NO hard-dated forcing function -- the thesis's central weakness. The next discrete events are ordinary earnings prints (Q2-2026 in ~July/August, screener ~48 days to next earnings) which can confirm the FCF/margin trajectory but are not re-rating catalysts. A genuine forcing function would require an UNANNOUNCED corporate action: a Prosegur Cash take-private/squeeze-out (Gubel already controls the parent; consolidating the listed Cash sub would collapse the holdco discount), a sizable buyback authorization, an asset sale of the Cash or Alarms stake, or activist entry -- none on the calendar as of 2026-06-13. Absent that, the re-rate depends on slow FCF-multiple convergence over multiple quarters. Honest answer for the director: WATCH the next 1-2 prints for FX-clean FCF confirmation and any capital-allocation pivot (buyback/Cash consolidation); enter on a flush, not on the catalyst, because the catalyst does not exist yet.
valley_of_death: The dominant 3-9 month risk is an ARS (secondarily USD) devaluation that flattens the reported P&L again -- Cash EBITA was already 'negatively impacted by Argentina macroeconomic conditions' in Q1-2026, and a sharp peso move would erase the reported growth narrative and likely the stock's recent strength even as organic numbers stay fine. Second, Security margin disappointment: management already guided that margin gains 'will be less significant over the course of the year', so a Q2/Q3 print showing the 2.8% EBITA margin stalling would puncture the 'structural margin transformation' thesis. Third, deleveraging is slow (~EUR28M/quarter) against EUR1.62B net debt; any working-capital reversal (the Q1 swing to +EUR8M operating cash flow from -EUR19M is seasonally fragile) could stall the debt-reduction story underpinning the equity-compounding case. No debt maturity wall (BBB, 60%+ fixed, EUR60M EIB loan just secured, EUR600M Cash bond already amortized), so the risk is earnings/FX, not refinancing. No cash-burn hump -- the business is FCF-positive.
positioning_washout: Limited mechanical-selling dynamics. The dividend (~EUR0.13/sh, ~4.9% yield, ~77% payout) is stable and uncut, so there is no yield-base liquidation trigger of the kind that washes out a dividend-cutter. The more relevant fact is the OPPOSITE of a washout: Gubel/Revoredo controls ~75%, so free float is thin (~25%) and illiquid -- which both caps forced selling AND means there is no large mechanical buyer to drive a re-rate. No index-inclusion catalyst, no forced-seller setup, no activist accumulation visible (insider buy ratio 0.00). The thin-float, controlled-company structure is double-edged: it floors downside (no big holder to dump) but removes any positioning-driven forcing function on the upside.
moderator_conclusion: PSG.MC is a high-quality forensic READ on a low-quality business with real but slow-burn cheapness and NO catalyst. The Interrogator scored it 4/5 credibility (STRENGTHENING) -- earnings +52.8%, FCF +53%, Alarms economics, deleveraging all genuinely improving, management confident and granular on what it controls. The Architect's SoP brackets EUR1.90 (strict parts, FX-impaired) to EUR3.60 (FCF-anchored bull); the reconciled base is ~EUR2.85, a thin ~6% premium to EUR2.68 once the deserved thin-margin (2.8% Security EBITA, 3.7% ROIC) and holdco/FX discounts are respected -- the screener's +75-81% Graham/IV15 MoS is optimistic. Applying the REGIME (CATALYST_WATCH_REGIME.md): the regime rewards hard-dated structural special-sits in thin-coverage distressed/deleveraging/forced-seller lanes and explicitly DEPRIORITIZES theses with no dated forcing function or that rely on slow multiple convergence. PSG has FIRED its only catalyst (Gubel tender, 2024, at EUR1.83 below today's price), Gubel already owns ~75% (thin float, no mechanical re-rate buyer), and there is no announced buyback/take-private/asset-sale -- so on the CATALYST axis the regime moves this DOWN. Verdict B (watchlist), conviction 2: do not chase at EUR2.68; the right entry is a capitulation flush (an ARS-devaluation-driven reported-revenue scare that knocks the price toward bear-SoP ~EUR1.90), where you would buy a deleveraging EUR218M-FCF machine at ~4x FCF with a ~6% yield. Separately, value_conviction 4: stripping out catalyst and regime, the VALUE case is strong -- a genuinely cheap (5.5x FCF), genuinely inflecting (+53% FCF/+53% NI), well-financed (BBB) franchise with high forensic credibility (4/5) trading near or below a defensible blended SoP; the only thing capping value_conviction at 4 not 5 is the structurally low ROIC/margin and FX fragility that make the discount partly deserved. The two scores diverge deliberately: cheap and clean (value 4), but no catalyst and out-of-favor lane (conviction 2).
sop_breakdown: Base case blends the FCF-anchored consolidated value (which captures the real EUR218M cash machine) against the strict segment-EBITA-minus-leverage parts (which respects the deserved thin-margin/holdco discount), then haircuts for FX and the absence of a catalyst. Parts at base multiples: CASH 74% stake ~EUR0.78B (between intrinsic EUR0.84B and market EUR0.72B); SECURITY ~EUR0.54B (7x ~EUR77M EBITA); ALARMS ~EUR0.36B (1.4x sales). Gross ~EUR1.68B less parent net debt EUR0.76B = EUR0.92B -> ~EUR1.70/sh on disaggregated parts; the consolidated FCF lens (EUR218M x ~8x = EUR1.74B equity -> EUR3.23/sh) pulls fair value up because Prosegur's value is in aggregate cash generation, not separable segments. Weighting the consolidated cash-machine view ~60% and strict parts ~40%, with a modest FX/holdco haircut, lands base-case fair value at EUR2.85/sh -- a slim ~6% premium to EUR2.68. Cheap, but the deserved discount eats most of the headline 75-81% model MoS.
risk_reward: Thin margin of safety, NOT asymmetric. Downside-to-break (bear SoP) EUR1.90 = -29% from EUR2.68; upside-to-base-fair EUR2.85 = +6%; upside-to-bull EUR3.60 = +34%. Base-case R:R ~0.2:1 (poor); bull-case R:R ~1.2:1. The screener's +75-81% Graham/IV15 MoS overstates the edge -- once the thin-margin/holdco/FX discount is respected, realistic upside-to-fair is single-digit at base and ~34% only in the no-FX-shock bull. A 'cheap with a thin margin of safety' name, not an asymmetric special-sit.
catalyst_status: FIRED. The only structural catalyst -- the Gubel, S.L. (Revoredo family) tender offer -- was authorized by CNMV on 2024-02-20 and completed 2024-03-18 at EUR1.83/share for ~13.23% of capital, lifting Gubel to ~74.8% ownership. That event is >2 years old AND priced BELOW today's EUR2.68, so the re-rate it could have driven is spent and went the wrong way for minorities. Verified as of 2026-06-13: NO live tender/squeeze-out, NO announced buyback ramp, NO Prosegur Cash take-private/delisting, NO asset sale, NO activist (insider buy ratio 0.00); the Q1-2026 call disclosed zero capital-action plans beyond routine deleveraging. The thesis rests entirely on FCF-multiple convergence with no dated forcing function.
peer_comps_note: Per peer_groups: true comps are global contract-security firms (Securitas SECU-B.ST, G4S/Allied Universal private) plus its own listed Cash sub (Prosegur Cash CASH.MC) and Brink's (BCO) for cash logistics. At 5.5x P/FCF, PSG screens cheap vs Securitas (~12-14x FCF) -- BUT ROIC 3.7% and net margin 2.4% mean the discount is partly EARNED (LatAm FX drag + structurally low-margin manned guarding). Sanity-check on the SoP multiple: the listed Cash sub trades at 8.9x EBITDA / EUR1.83B EV (vs Brink's ~6.5x), so the crown-jewel segment is NOT cheap on its own quote -- it is the consolidated holdco that carries the discount. Verdict cheap_vs_peers holds on FCF, but quality-adjusted the gap to Securitas is justified, not pure mispricing.
interrogator_dossier:
# Prosegur Compañía de Seguridad, S.A. (PSG.MC) — Forensic Dossier

*Interrogator dossier (Claude Opus 4.8). Source: Prosegur FY2025 results & press release (prosegur.com); Q4-2025 and Q1-2026 earnings calls/slides (Investing.com transcripts); company IR; CNMV history (Gubel tender offer). No FMP transcript was bundled; this dossier reasons from the screener financial trajectory cross-referenced against the most recent management commentary found on the web. Quotes are management phrasings reported in the Q1-2026 / Q4-2025 call coverage; numbers from the screener block are labeled (financials). Latest hard quarter: Q1 FY2026 (ended 31-Mar-2026).*

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

Prosegur is a three-engine physical-security conglomerate — **Security** (manned guarding + tech), **Cash** (cash-in-transit and cash management, held via the ~74% owned listed subsidiary Prosegur Cash, CASH.MC), and **Alarms** (residential/SME monitoring). The story management has told across the last several quarters is consistent and, unusually for a deep-value name, *improving*: a multi-year "value creation for shareholders" deleveraging-and-margin-recovery arc.

The arc's evolution: the older narrative was dominated by **LatAm FX as an existential drag** masking real volume growth. By FY2025 the framing shifted to "**organic growth is the truth, FX is noise**" — management leads every release with organic figures (FY2025 organic +10%, Q1-2026 organic +7.8%) and treats the reported number (FY2025 +0.5%, Q1-2026 +1.5%) as a translation artifact. The newest thread, introduced in Q1-2026, is **structural margin transformation**: in Cash, "transformation products now represent 36.4% of total Cash sales" (recurring, higher-margin services replacing volume-linked CIT); in Alarms, a deliberate pivot from "grow the base at any cost" to "**quality growth**" (churn cut from 95bps to 67bps, service margin 21%→25%). The Security narrative crossed a symbolic line in Q1-2026: "**Security reached cash flow breakeven for the first time in a first quarter**," and Cipher cybersecurity was folded into Security to sell "integrated physical and digital security."

Growth drivers quietly dropped: the old emphasis on top-line CIT volume in Cash is replaced by "transformation products" — a tacit admission that legacy cash logistics is ex-growth and the bet is mix-shift to services. Drivers added: US Security double-digit growth, Alarms unit economics, and balance-sheet deleveraging as the headline value driver.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

| Management claim | The hard evidence | Verdict |
|---|---|---|
| "Strong, broad-based growth" | Reported revenue FY2025 +0.5%, Q1-2026 +1.5% (financials/web). 3-yr CAGR +5.7%. Organic +10% / +7.8%. | **Half-true.** Organic strength is genuine; reported is near-zero because FX (ARS/USD) erases it. A shareholder is paid in reported euros, not organic. |
| "On the right track… generating value for shareholders" — earnings inflection | Net income €78M (2024) → €119M (2025), +52.8% (web/financials). EPS €0.15 → €0.22, +46.7% YoY. EBITA €357M, +8.9%. | **TRUE and material.** The load-bearing fact. Earnings power inflects hard while revenue is flat — operating leverage + tax-rate normalization (effective tax 42.0%, −449bps YoY). |
| Margin expansion | Net margin 2.4% (financials); gross 20.4% stable; consolidated EBITDA margin 11.1%, EBITA ~6.8%. Security EBITA margin 2.8%. | **Mixed.** Group margin is structurally thin (manned guarding is a 2–3% EBIT business). Expansion is real at the EBITA line (+8.9%) but off a low base; net margin remains razor-thin. |
| Deleveraging | Net debt €1.62B (financials, 2026-03-31); group reported €1.381B; Net debt/EBITDA 2.4x, −€28M QoQ; target <2.4x by year-end. | **TRUE, slow.** Deleveraging is happening at a modest pace; €1.6B net debt against €119M net income is still heavy leverage. |
| FCF recovery | TTM FCF €218M (financials); P/FCF 5.5x; FCF +53.3% YoY; Q1-2026 operating cash flow +€8M vs −€19M prior-year (web). | **TRUE.** Second load-bearing fact — FCF is real, growing, and 5.5x is genuinely cheap if sustainable. Q1 turning cash-positive is a structural seasonality break. |

Where the story is **richer than the numbers**: "value creation" framing implies a re-rating is owed; nothing in the reported top line (flat) supports a growth re-rate — only the earnings/FCF inflection does. Where it's **poorer**: management undersells how much of the EPS jump is tax-rate + operating leverage rather than demand, and how exposed it is to one more ARS devaluation.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

**Rising / Stable (confident, not defensive).** Across Q4-2025 and Q1-2026 the tone is offensive: "a strong first quarter," "solid operations performance," "broad-based growth across regions," "on the right track to comply with our main objective." Management volunteers point forward revenue figures (Q2 €1.44B, Q3 €1.49B) — a confidence tell; ex-growth managements give ranges, not points. Language is *operational and granular* (churn bps, transformation-product %, DSO) rather than macro-excuse-laden, a green flag for a LatAm-exposed name. The one defensive note is FX, handled with "robust risk management practices" but no quantified hedge disclosure.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

**Medium-High.** Financials show beats in 5 of 7 quarters (71%, financials) and FY2025 net profit "exceeding targets" (company headline). EPS delivery compounded +22.4% over 3 years and the +52.8% net-profit jump beat the company's own FY2025 objective. Management gives point revenue forecasts and broadly delivers — Q1-2026 net profit +15.2% continues the streak. The reliability gap is FX: management cannot guide reported numbers credibly (they don't control the peso), so they shifted goalposts to organic + earnings, where they deliver. **Score: Medium-High** on what they control; Low on reported-euro predictability.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

(1) **FX/earnings sensitivity** — pressed for quantified FX impact, management offered "robust risk management practices" with no hedge ratios (a non-answer). (2) **Security margin sustainability** — pushed on whether 2.8% EBITA keeps climbing, the CFO conceded improvements "will be less significant over the course of the year" — the easy margin gains are behind. (3) **Cash/Argentina** — Cash EBITA flat, "negatively impacted by Argentina macroeconomic conditions," the structural soft underbelly. What analysts are NOT pushing on but should: the absence of any catalyst to close the holdco/SoP discount (no buyback ramp, no Cash take-private, no asset sale).

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

🟢 Net income +52.8% FY2025, EPS +46.7% — a genuine earnings inflection, not engineering (corroborated by EBITA +8.9% + tax normalization).
🟢 FCF €218M TTM at 5.5x P/FCF; Q1-2026 turned operating-cash-positive (+€8M vs −€19M).
🟢 Alarms unit economics improving (churn 95→67bps, service margin 21%→25%).
🟢 Deleveraging on track (2.4x, −€28M QoQ), BBB rated, 60%+ debt fixed at 2.9% avg cost.
🔴 Net margin 2.4%, Security EBITA margin 2.8%, ROIC 3.7% (financials) — structurally low quality; the business *deserves* a discount.
🔴 Reported revenue flat (+0.5% FY, +1.5% Q1) — the growth story lives in "organic," which FX repeatedly eats.
🔴 €1.6B net debt is heavy; Argentina/ARS is a recurring, uncontrollable hit to Cash.
🔴 **No catalyst.** The only structural event — Gubel's €1.83/share tender — closed March 2024 *below* today's €2.68. No live buyback ramp, no Cash take-private, no asset sale, no activist. Insider buy ratio 0.00 (financials).

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

The terminology shift from "volume" to "**transformation products**" in Cash is the key tell — it concedes legacy CIT is in secular decline and reframes the bet as a services/mix story (margin over volume). The Cipher-into-Security integration signals Prosegur wants to be sold as an *integrated* security platform, not a labor-arbitrage guard company — a multiple-aspiration move, though the 2.8% margin says the market won't pay for it yet. The ritual invocation of "value for shareholders" without any accompanying capital-return action (flat ~€0.13/sh dividend, ~4.9% yield, no buyback acceleration) reads as aspiration, not plan — management knows the stock is cheap and is signaling, but holds no forcing function.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

**STRENGTHENING (operationally) / STABLE (structurally).** The operating trajectory is genuinely improving — earnings, FCF, Alarms economics, deleveraging all point the right way, and management is credible and confident on what it controls. But this is a *quality-improving-but-still-low-quality* compounder with **no catalyst to force the discount closed**. The single most important thing the director must weigh: **this is a pure margin-of-safety / FCF-multiple bet, not a special situation.** The €1.83 Gubel tender already fired (below today's price), Gubel already owns ~75%, and there is no announced buyback ramp, Cash take-private, or asset sale to act as a forcing function. You are paid 5.5x FCF and a ~4.9% dividend to wait for a re-rate that has no dated trigger and is perpetually one ARS devaluation away from being erased on the reported line.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING
